matches.name: Dollar Tree
matches.description: Filing emphasizes buying power and supply-chain investments ("substantial buying power", "investments in technology", automatic replenishment and distribution center expansion) and rising selling square footage from ~30.3M to 40.5M, supporting a scale-based advantage that appears stable and reinforc

matches.name: Costco
matches.description: The filing repeatedly attributes low-price ability to "high sales volumes and rapid inventory turnover" and efficient distribution (depots, cross-docking), plus recurring membership fees and a high Executive-member penetration that reinforce volume economics and pricerelated advantages.

matches.name: Dollar General
matches.description: The company emphasizes low‑cost operating structure, extensive distribution network (11 distribution centers plus leased space), and purchasing power from a large store base ('attractive store economics' and 'strong purchasing power'), which create reinforcing scale advantages across sourcing, distr

matches.name: Hershey Company (The)
matches.description: The company states that the “foundation of our marketing strategy is our strong brand equities,” invests heavily in advertising and product innovation, and repeatedly leverages those brands across geographies and product lines—indicating a durable, reinforcing brand advantage.

matches.name: Coca-Cola Company (The)
matches.description: The Company emphasizes Trademark Coca‑Cola and other global trademarks and a worldwide bottling system; the proxy and 10‑K highlight pervasive brand franchises and an "unrivaled distribution system," suggesting a durable, reinforcing brand advantage across markets.

matches.name: PepsiCo
matches.description: The filing emphasizes durable trademarks and brands (long list of household names) and "go-to-market systems and strong brands," along with scale and integrated portfolio benefits ("Power of One"), implying a stable, reinforcing brand-based advantage across markets.
